conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Does Alex normally live with you, or are you the person responsible for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex does live with me normally, but I should mention that they've been in local authority care for a little while now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I need to understand the situation a bit more to assess your eligibility properly.

How long has Alex been in local authority care - is it less than 8 weeks, or has it been 8 weeks or longer?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been a bit longer than that - about 12 weeks now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I need to ask one more important question:

During these 12 weeks that Alex has been in local authority care, have you been contributing at least the same amount towards Alex's costs as the amount of Child Benefit you would receive?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I haven't been able to contribute towards Alex's costs during this time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex at this time.

Here's why: While Alex is the right age (8 years old) and normally lives with you, the fact that Alex has been in local authority care for 12 weeks affects your eligibility. According to the Child Benefit rules, when a child has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit if you're contributing at least as much towards the child's costs as the Child Benefit amount you would receive.

Since you haven't been able to contribute towards Alex's costs during this time, you don't meet the eligibility criteria while Alex remains in care under these circumstances.

If your situation changes - for example, if Alex returns to live with you, or if you start contributing the required amount towards Alex's costs - your eligibility could change, and you would be able to apply or reapply for Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old and normally lives with the claimant, which would ordinarily satisfy the basic eligibility criteria. However, Alex has been in local authority care for 12 weeks. According to the gov.uk guidance on child benefit for children in care, if a child has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks, Child Benefit can only continue if the claimant contributes at least as much towards the child's costs as the Child Benefit amount they would receive. The claimant has confirmed they have not been able to contribute towards Alex's costs during this time. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex while these circumstances continue.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who normally lives with them but has been in local authority care for 12 weeks. While the basic age and relationship criteria are met, the child has been in care for longer than 8 weeks and the claimant has not been contributing towards the child's costs at a level equal to or greater than the Child Benefit amount. Under these circumstances, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
